mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, invalidating the recession fear premium mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and capital demand channels, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p>0.10) in multivariate regression over a 5-year sample, or joint expansion yields negative alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates into short-term positive S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during inflationary supply shocks, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210.0, contradicting the tightening premium hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of non-farm payrolls increasing by more than 200k and the unemployment rate declining confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Bivariate interaction effect yields negative alpha after controlling for market beta, or joint signal fails to beat baseline in >50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized provides a fundamental floor for corporate earnings expansion, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a 10-year sample, refuting the earnings floor mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals a sharp contraction in the supply sector, triggering recession risk premiums that immediately suppress S&P 500 valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >65% of instances following a >1.0% INDPRO decline, indicating the contraction signal is noise rather than a recession precursor.
